import type { FeatureId } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\n2: import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"./types\";\n3: \n4: export interface OptInFeatureConfig {\n5:   slug: FeatureId;\n6:   titleI18nKey: string;\n7:   descriptionI18nKey: string;\n8:   policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\n9:   /** Scopes where this feature can be configured. Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n10:   scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n11: }\n12: \n13: /** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\n14: export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n15: \n16: /**\n17:  * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n18:  * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n19:  */\n20: export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n21:   // Example - to be populated with actual features\n22:   // {\n23:   //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n24:   //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n25:   //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n26:   //   policy: \"permissive\",\n27:   //   scope: [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\n28:   // },\n29: ];\n30: \n31: /**\n32:  * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\n33:  */\n34: export function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\n35:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\n36: }\n37: \n38: /**\n39:  * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\n40:  * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n41:  */\n42: export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n43:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n44: }\n45: \n46: /**\n47:  * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n48:  */\n49: export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n50: \n51: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\n52: export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n53: \n54: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\n55: export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n56: \n57: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\n58: export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n59: \n60: /**\n61:  * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n62:  * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n63:  */\n64: export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n65:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n66: }\n67: \n68: /**\n69:  * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n70:  * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n71:  * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n72:  */\n73: export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\n74:   const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\n75:   if (!config) return false;\n76:   return !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\n77: }\n78: import { z } from \\\"zod\\\";\\n3: \\n4: import { getFeatureOptInService } from \\\"@calcom/features/di/containers/FeatureOptInService\\\";\\n5: import { TeamRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/ee/teams/repositories/TeamRepository\\\";\\n6: import { isOptInFeature } from \\\"@calcom/features/feature-opt-in/config\\\";\\n7: import { FeaturesRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\\\";\\n8: import { MembershipRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/membership/repositories/MembershipRepository\\\";\\n9: import { prisma } from \\\"@calcom/prisma\\\";\\n10: \\n11: import { TRPCError } from \\\"@trpc/server\\\";\\n12: \\n13: import authedProcedure from \\\"../../../procedures/authedProcedure\\\";\\n14: import { router } from \\\"../../../trpc\\\";\\n15: import { createOrgPbacProcedure, createTeamPbacProcedure } from \\\"../../../procedures/pbacProcedures\\\";\\n16: \\n17: const featureStateSchema: ZodEnum<[\\\"enabled\\\", \\\"disabled\\\", \\\"inherit\\\"]> = z.enum([\\\"enabled\\\", \\\"disabled\\\", \\\"inherit\\\"]);\\n18: \\n19: const featureOptInService = getFeatureOptInService();\\n20: const featuresRepository = new FeaturesRepository(prisma);\\n21: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\\n22: \\n23: /**\\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\\n26:  */\\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\\n28:   const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\\n29:     userId,\\n30:     filters: { accepted: true },\\n31:   });\\n32: \\n33:   let orgId: number | null = null;\\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\\n35: \\n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\\n39:     } else {\\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\\n41:     }\\n42:   }\\n43: \\n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\\n48:   /**\\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\\n50:    * This considers all teams/orgs the user belongs to.\\n51:    */\\n52:   listForUser: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n53:     const { orgId, teamIds } = await getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id);\\n54: \\n55:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser({\\n56:       userId: ctx.user.id,\\n57:       orgId,\\n58:       teamIds,\\n59:     });\\n60:   }),\\n61: \\n62:   /**\\n63:    * Get all opt-in features with states for a team settings page.\\n64:    * Used by team admins to configure feature opt-in for their team.\\n65:    * Also returns the organization state if the team belongs to an organization.\\n66:    */\\n67:   listForTeam: createTeamPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ input }) => {\\n68:     // Get the team's parent organization ID (if any)\\n69:     const parentOrg = await teamRepository.findParentOrganizationByTeamId(input.teamId);\\n70:     const parentOrgId = parentOrg?.id ?? null;\\n71: \\n72:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: input.teamId, parentOrgId, scope: \\\"team\\\" });\\n73:   }),\\n74: \\n75:   /**\\n76:    * Get all opt-in features with states for organization settings page.\\n77:    * Used by org admins to configure feature opt-in for their organization.\\n78:    */\\n79:   listForOrganization: createOrgPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n80:     // Organizations use the same listFeaturesForTeam since they're stored in TeamFeatures\\n81:     // Pass scope: \\\"org\\\" to filter features that are scoped to organizations\\n82:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: ctx.organizationId, scope: \\\"org\\\" });\\n83:   }),\\n84: \\n85:   /**\\n86:    * Set user's feature state.\\n87:    */\\n88:   setUserState: authedProcedure\\n89:     .input(\\n90:       z.object({\\n91:         slug: z.string(),\\n92:         state: featureStateSchema,\\n93:       })\\n94:     )\\n95:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n96:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n97:         throw new TRPCError({\\n98:           code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n99:           message: \\\"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\\\",\\n100:         });\\n101:       }\\n102: \\n103:       await featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\\n104:         userId: ctx.user.id,\\n105:         featureId: input.slug,\\n106:         state: input.state,\\n107:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\\n108:       });\\n109: \\n110:       return { success: true };\\n111:     }),\\n112: \\n113:   /**\\n114:    * Set team's feature state (requires team admin).\\n115:    */\\n116:   setTeamState: createTeamPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.update\\\")\\n117:     .input(\\n118:       z.object({\\n119:         slug: z.string(),\\n120:         state: featureStateSchema,\\n121:       })\\n122:     )\\n123:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n124:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n125:         throw new TRPCError({\\n126:           code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n127:           message: \\\"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\\\",\\n128:         });\\n129:       }\\n130: \\n131:       await featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\\n132:         teamId: input.teamId,\\n133:         featureId: input.slug,\\n134:         state: input.state,\\n135:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\\n136:         scope: \\\"team\\\",\\n137:       });\\n138: \\n139:       return { success: true };\\n140:     }),\\n141: \\n142:   /**\\n143:    * Set organization's feature state (requires org admin).\\n144:    */\\n145:   setOrganizationState: createOrgPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.update\\\")\\n146:     .input(\\n147:       z.object({\\n148:         slug: z.string(),\\n149:         state: featureStateSchema,\\n150:       })\\n151:     )\\n152:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n153:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n154:         throw new TRPCError({\\n155:           code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n156:           message: \\\"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\\\",\\n157:         });\\n158:       }\\n159: \\n160:       // Organizations use the same TeamFeatures table\\n161:       await featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\\n162:         teamId: ctx.organizationId,\\n163:         featureId: input.slug,\\n164:         state: input.state,\\n165:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\\n166:         scope: \\\"org\\\",\\n167:       });\\n168: \\n169:       return { success: true };\\n170:     }),\\n171: \\n172:   /**\\n173:    * Get user's auto opt-in preference.\\n174:    */\\n175:   getUserAutoOptIn: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n176:     const autoOptIn = await featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(ctx.user.id);\\n177:     return { autoOptIn };\\n178:   }),\\n179: \\n180:   /**\\n181:    * Set user's auto opt-in preference.\\n182:    */\\n183:   setUserAutoOptIn: authedProcedure\\n184:     .input(\\n185:       z.object({\\n186: return { autoOptIn: result[input.teamId] ?? false };\\n200:   }),\\n201: \\n202:   /**\\n203:    * Set team's auto opt-in preference (requires team admin).\\n204:    */\\n205:   setTeamAutoOptIn: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\n22: \n23: /**\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\n26:  */\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\n28:   const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\n29:     userId,\n30:     filters: { accepted: true },\n31:   });\n32: \n33:   let orgId: number | null = null;\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\n35: \n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\n39:     } else {\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\n41:     }\n42:   }\n43: \n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\n45: }\n46: \n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\n48:   /**\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\n50:    * This considers all teams/orgs the user belongs to.\n51:    */\n52:   listForUser: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\n53:     const { orgId, teamIds } = await getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id);\n54: \n55:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser({\n56:       userId: ctx.user.id,\n57:       orgId,\n58:       teamIds,\n59:     });\n60:   }),\n61: \n62:   /**\n63:    * Get all opt-in features with states for a team settings page.\n64:    * Used by team admins to configure feature opt-in for their team.\n65:    * Also returns the organization state if the team belongs to an organization.\n66:    */\n67:   listForTeam: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.read\").query(async ({ input }) => {\n68:     // Get the team's parent organization ID (if any)\n69:     const parentOrg = await teamRepository.findParentOrganizationByTeamId(input.teamId);\n70:     const parentOrgId = parentOrg?.id ?? null;\n71: \n72:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: input.teamId, parentOrgId, scope: \"team\" });\n73:   }),\n74: \n75:   /**\n76:    * Get all opt-in features with states for organization settings {\n80:     // Organizations use the same listFeaturesForTeam since they're stored in TeamFeatures\n81:     // Pass scope: \"org\" to filter features that are scoped to organizations\n82:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: ctx.organizationId, scope: \"org\" });\n83:   }),\n84: \n85:   /**\n86:    * Set user's feature state.\n87:    */\n88:   setUserState: {\n96:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n97:         throw new TRPCError({\n98:           code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n99:           message: \"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\",\n100:         });\n101:       }\n102: \n103:       await featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\n104:         userId: ctx.user.id,\n105:         featureId: input.slug,\n106:         state: input.state,\n107:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n108:       });\n109: \n110:       return { success: true };\n111:     }),\n112: \n113:   /**\n114:    * Set team's feature state (requires team admin).\n115:    */\n116:   setTeamState: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n117:     .input(\n118:       z.object({\n119:         slug: z.string(),\n120:         state: featureStateSchema,\n121:       })\n122:     )\n123:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n124:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n125:         throw new TRPCError({\n126:           code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n127:           message: \"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\",\n128:         });\n129:       }\n130: \n131:       await featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\n132:         teamId: input.teamId,\n133:         featureId: input.slug,\n134:         state: input.state,\n135:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n136:         scope: \"team\",\n137:       });\n138: \n139:       return { success: true };\n140:     }),\n141: \n142:   /**\n143:    * Set organization's feature state (requires org admin).\n144:    */\n145:   setOrganizationState: createOrgPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n146:     .input(\n147:       z.object({\n148:         slug: z.string(),\n149:         state: featureStateSchema,\n150:       })\n151:     )\n152:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n153:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n154:         throw new TRPCError({\n155:           code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n156:           message: \"Invalid slug. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AQUMVzzG7PukPtTnmWBsAu","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: import type { EffectiveStateReason } from \\\"@calcom/features/feature-opt-in/lib/computeEffectiveState\\\";\\n2: import type { FeatureState } from \\\"@calcom/features/flags/config\\\";\\n3: \\n4: /**\\n5:  * Policy that determines how feature opt-in states are evaluated.\\n6:  *\\n7:  * - `permissive`: User opt-in can activate the feature; any explicit enable above is sufficient;\\n8:  *   disables only win if ALL teams disable.\\n9:  * - `strict`: User opt-in alone is not enough; requires explicit enable from org/team;\\n10:  *   ANY explicit disable blocks.\\n11:  */\\n12: export type OptInFeaturePolicy = \\\"permissive\\\" | \\\"strict\\\";\\n13: \\n14: /**\\n15:  * Scope that determines at which levels a feature can be configured.\\n16:  *\\n17:  * - `org`: Feature can be configured at the organization level\\n18:  * - `team`: Feature can be configured at the team level\\n19:  * - `user`: Feature can be configured at the user level\\n20:  */\\n21: export type OptInFeatureScope = \\\"org\\\" | \\\"team\\\" | \\\"user\\\";\\n22: \\n23: /**\\n24:  * Normalized feature representation used across all scopes (user, team, org).\\n25:  */\\n26: export interface NormalizedFeature {\\n27:   slug: string;\\n28:   globalEnabled: boolean;\\n29:   /** The current state value for this scope (userState, teamState, etc.) */\\n30:   currentState: FeatureState;\\n31:   /** The reason for the effective state (only available for user scope) */\\n32:   effectiveReason?: EffectiveStateReason;\\n33: }\\n34: \\n35: /**\\n36:  * Toggle option labels - {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_018HRccnZTPWviAAJeEzA4cU","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I have a clearer picture. Let me analyze the issues:\n\n## Bug 1: Type Error in Router - setUserState (line 88-111)\n\nThe `setUserFeatureState` method has this type signature:\n```typescript\nasync setUserFeatureState(\n  input:\n    | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n    | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\" }\n): Promise<void>;\n```\n\nThe union type specifies that `assignedBy` is only included when state is \"enabled\" or \"disabled\", NOT when state is \"inherit\".\n\nBut in the router (line 103-108):\n```typescript\nawait featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\n  userId: ctx.user.id,\n  featureId: input.slug,\n  state: input.state,  // Can be \"enabled\" | \"disabled\" | \"inherit\"\n  assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n});\n```\n\nThis always passes `assignedBy`, even when `state` is \"inherit\", which violates the type union. The router should conditionally pass `assignedBy` only when `state` is not \"inherit\".\n\n## Bug 2: Type Error in Router - setTeamState (line 116-140)\n\nSame issue as Bug 1. The input object passes `assignedBy` unconditionally, even when state is \"inherit\":\n\n```typescript\nawait featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\n  teamId: input.teamId,\n  featureId: input.slug,\n  state: input.state,\n  assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n  scope: \"team\",\n});\n```\n\nShould check if state is \"inherit\" before passing assignedBy.\n\n## Bug 3: Type Error in Router - setOrganizationState (line 145-170)\n\n\nThe `setOrganizationFeatureState` call has the same problem—it unconditionally includes `assignedBy` regardless of whether state is \"inherit\", breaking the type contract.\n\n## Bug 4: Compliance Issue - Missing Scope Validation in Router (line 96-101)\n\nThe `setUserState` router endpoint checks if the slug is in the OPT_IN_FEATURES list using `isOptInFeature()`, but it doesn't validate that the feature is scoped to \"user\". A feature could be configured for \"team\" or \"org\" scope only but still pass this check. The same validation gap exists in the team and organization endpoints—they should verify scope matches before calling the service methods. The service method accepts `assignedBy` as a number in the union type, but then passes it to the repository as `assignedBy: `user_${assignedBy}`` - converting it to a string. This type mismatch between the service layer (number) and repository layer (string) is a real bug that needs fixing. The repository expects a string identifier, so either the service should accept a string or perform the conversion more explicitly. Looking at how this flows through the codebase, the assignedBy parameter needs consistent typing across all layers to prevent runtime errors. );\n\n  return { success: true };\n})\n```\n\nThe same pattern needs to be applied to `setTeamState` and `setOrganizationState` mutations. Both should conditionally construct the input object based on whether state is \"inherit\", ensuring the type system receives the correct union type in each case.\n\nThere's also a potential issue with the `isOptInFeature` validation—it checks if a slug exists in the opt-in features list, but doesn't verify the scope matches the operation being performed (user vs team vs organization).\n\nThe core functional bugs are the three state-setting mutations that unconditionally pass `assignedBy` regardless of the state value, violating the type union contract defined in the service layer.\n\nLooking at `setTeamFeatureState`, the method accepts a union type where `\"inherit\"` state shouldn't include `assignedBy`, yet the implementation always provides it. The scope validation happens first, checking if the feature is permitted at the specified scope level, then it branches based on the state value.\n\nWhen state is `\"inherit\"`, only `teamId`, `featureId`, and `state` get passed to the repository. Otherwise, it extracts `assignedBy` from the input and formats it as `user-${assignedBy}` before passing it along with the other parameters. This structure correctly handles the type constraint that `assignedBy` should only be present for enabled/disabled states, not for inherit. The router passes `input.slug` as a string, but `setUserFeatureState` requires a `FeatureId` type. The `isOptInFeature` type guard should narrow `input.slug` to `FeatureId` within the conditional block, but there's a mismatch—the guard checks the parameter type, not the actual variable being passed. This means TypeScript won't recognize `input.slug` as narrowed to `FeatureId` when it's passed to the function, causing a type error.\n\nLooking at the actual implementation, the core issues are that `setUserState`, `setTeamState`, and `setOrganizationState` all pass `assignedBy` regardless of whether the state is \"inherit\"—they should only include it when assigning a specific feature state, not when clearing it.\n\nNow examining `listFeaturesForTeam` starting at line 216, it fetches all features and team states in parallel, then maps over scoped features to find matching global feature data and team-specific overrides.\n\nThe method filters results to only return globally enabled features. However, looking at the router's `listForTeam` procedure around line 67, there's a discrepancy in how the parent organization ID is being handled between the two implementations. The organization endpoint correctly passes `scope: \"org\"` to filter for organization-scoped features, while the team endpoint uses `scope: \"team\"`. Both leverage the same underlying service method but with different scope values to distinguish between team and organization feature sets. Looking at the setTeamState and setOrganizationState procedures, they similarly call `setTeamFeatureState` with appropriate scope parameters—the service method is designed to handle both contexts. I'm examining the input validation for the scope parameter in the router to understand how it's being typed and validated. The createTeamPbacProcedure likely injects teamId into the input automatically based on the naming convention, so `input.teamId` should be available at runtime. Assuming that's correct, the actual issues are that all three state-setting procedures (user, team, and organization) pass `assignedBy` unconditionally, even when the state is \"inherit\"—which violates the type schema that requires `assignedBy` to be absent in that case.\n\nI need to verify the return type flow. Looking at `listFeaturesForUser`, it returns `ListFeaturesForUserResult[]`, which includes `featureId`, `globalEnabled`, `orgState`, and `teamState` fields. The method filters results by `globalEnabled`, so only enabled features are returned to the caller.\n\nThe `resolvedStates` object is typed as `Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>`, and each mapped element should match the `ListFeaturesForUserResult` structure. I should check the `ResolvedFeatureState` type definition to confirm it aligns with what's being returned.\n\nLooking at the type, `ResolvedFeatureState` contains `featureId`, `globalEnabled`, `orgState`, `teamStates`, `userState`, `effectiveEnabled`, `effectiveReason`, `orgAutoOptIn`, `teamAutoOptIns`, and `userAutoOptIn` - this matches the expected return type exactly.\n\nNow I need to trace through the router's `listForUser` method to see if there are any issues with how it retrieves the org and team IDs from the context.\n\nThe method calls `getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id)` and passes the resulting `orgId` and `teamIds` to `featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser()`. The service then filters to only user-scoped features via `getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\")`, which guarantees the returned features have the correct scope.\n\nThe main issues I've identified so far are the `assignedBy` type mismatches in the router responses. Let me focus on identifying and fixing those.\n\nLooking at the `setUserFeatureState` input type more carefully—it's a discriminated union where the `state` field determines the shape. When `state` is \"inherit\", `assignedBy` shouldn't be included. When it's \"enabled\" or \"disabled\", `assignedBy` is required. But in the router, `assignedBy: ctx.user.id` gets passed unconditionally regardless of the state value, which violates the union constraint when state is \"inherit\".\n\nThe solution is to conditionally build the object based on state. If state equals \"inherit\", omit the `assignedBy` field entirely. Otherwise, include it along with the specific state value. The isFeatureAllowedForScope function (lines 73-77) also looks correct. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EVnCrrfyPScpBPzgUZrbvB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I've identified and documented three critical bugs, all related to type mismatches in the router file. The issues are all in the feature state mutation endpoints where the router always passes the `assignedBy` parameter, even when the state is \"inherit\".\n\nAccording to the type signatures defined in the service interface (IFeatureOptInService.ts), the `assignedBy` field should only be included in the input object when the state is \"enabled\" or \"disabled\", NOT when it's \"inherit\". This creates a discriminated union where the presence of `assignedBy` is conditional on the state value.\n\nThe current router implementation violates this contract by unconditionally passing `assignedBy` for all state values, which would cause TypeScript compilation errors when the code is type-checked.\n\nAll three mutations (setUserState, setTeamState, setOrganizationState) have the same bug and need the same fix: conditionally construct the input object based on the state value, only including `assignedBy` when appropriate.\n\nThese are critical bugs because:\n1. They would prevent the code from compiling with proper TypeScript strict checking\n2. They represent a deviation from the specified type contract in the service interface\n3. They could cause runtime issues if the service implementation relies on the type discrimination\n\nThe fixes provided wrap the service calls in conditional logic that matches the type union properly.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2250,"cache_read_input_tokens":55344,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2250},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ebb80917-6587-4c56-add1-58c5c09f675a","uuid":"f9c929d9-a262-42c6-b4fc-ccf3bc3438ae"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EVnCrrfyPScpBPzgUZrbvB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! I've completed my code review and identified three critical defects. Here's a summary of the findings:\n\n## Summary of Defects Found\n\nAll three defects are **type mismatches in the tRPC router** that violate the service interface contract:\n\n### Critical Bugs (3)\n\n1. **setUserState mutation (line 103)**: Always passes `assignedBy` even when state is `\"inherit\"`, violating the discriminated union type that specifies `assignedBy` should only be present for `\"enabled\"` or `\"disabled\"` states.\n\n2. **setTeamState mutation (line 131)**: Same type mismatch - unconditionally passes `assignedBy` regardless of the state value.\n\n3. **setOrganizationState mutation (line 161)**: Same type mismatch affecting the organization settings endpoint.\n\n### Root Cause\n\nThe service interface defines a discriminated union for the `setUserFeatureState` and `setTeamFeatureState` methods:\n```typescript\ninput:\n  | { ...; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n  | { ...; state: \"inherit\" }  // NO assignedBy\n```\n\nThe router violates this contract by always including `assignedBy` in the input object, causing TypeScript compilation errors.\n\n### Impact\n\n- **Severity**: Critical - These are type violations that prevent the code from compiling with strict TypeScript checking\n- **Functional Impact**: The service implementation correctly handles the type discrimination, but the router bypasses it, creating a type safety gap\n- **Compliance**: Deviates from the established interface contract\n\nAll fixes are provided as unified diffs in `/workspace/review.json` and conditionally construct the input object based on state value, ensuring type safety."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2250,"cache_read_input_tokens":55344,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2250},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ebb80917-6587-4c56-add1-58c5c09f675a","uuid":"0bde7db4-cd26-4b75-b179-0b2f9dab9d54"}
